articles.title: Microsoft Warns of WhatsApp Attachments Spreading Backdoor on Windows PCs
articles.summary: Microsoft has identified a malicious campaign distributing VBS malware via WhatsApp attachments, which allows attackers to gain unauthorized remote access to compromised Windows systems.

articles.title: Residential proxies evaded IP reputation checks in 78% of 4B sessions
articles.summary: Residential proxies are significantly undermining IP reputation systems by masking malicious traffic as legitimate user activity in the majority of analyzed sessions. This trend poses a severe challenge for security controls that rely on IP-based filtering to block threats.

articles.title: Medtech giant Stryker says it’s back up after Iranian cyberattack
articles.summary: Medical technology company Stryker has restored operations following a wiper malware attack orchestrated by the Iranian-linked group Handala. The incident caused significant operational disruption for the global firm last month.

articles.title: Software supply chain hacks trigger wave of intrusions, data theft
articles.summary: A wave of software supply chain attacks, including the Axios and Trivy breaches, has resulted in hundreds of thousands of stolen secrets that pose a significant risk of downstream customer compromises and ransomware. Security researchers warn that these intrusions, linked to North Korean actors and TeamPCP, could facilitate widespread future malicious activity.

articles.title: OpenSSH 10.3 patches five security bugs and drops legacy rekeying support
articles.summary: OpenSSH 10.3 has been released to address five security vulnerabilities while simultaneously removing legacy code that supported non-rekeying SSH implementations. This change will cause connectivity issues for outdated SSH clients and servers that do not comply with modern rekeying standards.

articles.title: Yurei Ransomware Uses Common Tools, Adds Stranger Things References
articles.summary: The Yurei ransomware campaign leverages standard administrative tools and thematic payload names inspired by 'Stranger Things' to compromise and encrypt targeted systems. Security researchers at Team Cymru have identified these tactics, highlighting the group's reliance on common utilities for malicious operations.

articles.title: Threat actor UAC-0255 impersonate CERT-UA to spread AGEWHEEZE malware via phishing
articles.summary: The threat actor UAC-0255 is targeting users through a massive phishing campaign that impersonates CERT-UA to distribute the AGEWHEEZE malware disguised as a security tool. The campaign involves sending emails to approximately one million recipients, prompting them to download and execute malicious archives.

articles.title: Adversaries Exploit Vacant Homes to Intercept Mail in Hybrid Cybercrime
articles.summary: Threat actors are leveraging vacant properties as physical drop addresses to intercept mail, facilitating identity theft and broader fraud schemes. This hybrid attack method highlights how physical exploitation supports cyber-enabled criminal activities.

articles.title: New Progress ShareFile flaws can be chained in pre-auth RCE attacks
articles.summary: Two vulnerabilities in Progress ShareFile can be chained by attackers to achieve pre-authentication remote code execution and unauthorized file exfiltration. This flaw poses a significant risk to enterprise environments using the secure file transfer solution.

articles.title: Medtech giant Stryker fully operational after data-wiping attack
articles.summary: Medical technology company Stryker has restored operations three weeks after a destructive cyberattack that wiped out company systems, an incident claimed by the Iranian-linked hacktivist group Handala.

articles.title: Storm Infostealer Sold as Service, Targets Browsers, Wallets and Accounts
articles.summary: Varonis Threat Labs identified the Storm infostealer, a new Malware-as-a-Service (MaaS) platform designed to exfiltrate data from browsers, cryptocurrency wallets, and user accounts by bypassing Google Chrome's encryption. This sophisticated tool allows threat actors to easily deploy malicious operations targeting sensitive credentials.

articles.title: New Red Hat subscription simplifies long-term enterprise Linux support
articles.summary: Red Hat has introduced a new 14-year Extended Life Cycle Premium subscription for Red Hat Enterprise Linux to provide long-term, stable support for enterprise workloads. This offering aims to simplify infrastructure management for organizations maintaining critical, change-averse systems.

articles.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Pre-Auth Chains, Android Rootkits, CloudTrail Evasion & 10 More Stories
articles.summary: This bulletin provides a concise roundup of current cybersecurity risks, highlighting emerging trends like pre-authentication exploit chains, Android rootkits, and cloud evasion techniques. It focuses on the practical, fast-moving landscape of system vulnerabilities and threat actor activity.

articles.title: Researchers Uncover Mining Operation Using ISO Lures to Spread RATs and Crypto Miners
articles.summary: A threat actor codenamed REF1695 is using malicious ISO files as lures to distribute remote access trojans and cryptocurrency miners while conducting CPA fraud. The campaign has been active since November 2023, targeting users through fake software installation pages.

articles.title: The State of Trusted Open Source Report
articles.summary: The report provides an analysis of open source consumption patterns, including common vulnerabilities and maintenance practices across container images and software libraries.

articles.title: DarkSword exploit forces Apple to loosen its patching policy
articles.summary: Apple has modified its security update strategy by providing patches to older iOS 18 devices to defend against the DarkSword and Coruna exploit kits. This shift marks a notable departure from the company's traditional requirement for users to upgrade to the latest OS version to receive critical fixes.

articles.title: Critical Cisco IMC auth bypass gives attackers Admin access
articles.summary: Cisco has released patches for multiple high-severity flaws, including a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in its Integrated Management Controller that grants attackers unauthorized administrative access.

articles.title: Why GitHub Developers Are Targeted by Token Giveaway Scams
articles.summary: GitHub developers are increasingly being targeted by cryptocurrency giveaway scams that leverage fake rewards to steal credentials or assets. Users are advised to exercise caution by verifying repository maintainers and avoiding suspicious links.

articles.title: WhatsApp Alerts 200 Users After Fake iOS App Installed Spyware; Italian Firm Faces Action
articles.summary: Meta has notified approximately 200 WhatsApp users in Italy that they were targeted by a social engineering campaign involving a malicious, spyware-infected version of the iOS application. The activity is currently linked to an Italian firm, prompting further scrutiny.

articles.title: Microsoft links Classic Outlook issue to email delivery problems
articles.summary: Microsoft is investigating a technical issue in Classic Outlook that is preventing some users from successfully sending emails through Outlook.com. The company is currently working to resolve this service disruption.

articles.title: TrueConf zero-day vulnerability exploited to target government networks
articles.summary: State-sponsored attackers exploited a zero-day vulnerability in TrueConf videoconferencing software to deploy malware against government networks in Southeast Asia. The attack compromised the application's trusted update mechanism to bypass security controls in restricted environments.

articles.title: Over 14,000 F5 BIG-IP APM instances still exposed to RCE attacks
articles.summary: Shadowserver reports that over 14,000 F5 BIG-IP APM instances remain exposed to ongoing exploitation attempts targeting a critical remote code execution vulnerability.

articles.title: The company's biggest security hole lived in the breakroom
articles.summary: The headline implies that a significant security vulnerability originated from human behavior or physical assets located within an office breakroom, suggesting an insider threat or poor physical security hygiene. It highlights how non-digital spaces can serve as critical vectors for compromising corporate infrastructure.

articles.title: Italian spyware vendor creates Fake WhatsApp app, targeting 200 users
articles.summary: WhatsApp identified and blocked a malicious, fake version of its application created by Italian firm SIO/Asigint that was used to deploy spyware against approximately 200 users. The company is now urging affected users to reinstall the official application to mitigate the threat.

articles.title: Apple Expands iOS 18.7.7 Update to More Devices to Block DarkSword Exploit
articles.summary: Apple has expanded the availability of iOS and iPadOS 18.7.7 to additional devices to mitigate security risks associated with the DarkSword exploit kit. The update is being pushed to ensure users are protected against potential exploitation.

articles.title: Trust, friction, and ROI: A CISO’s take on making security work for the business
articles.summary: This interview with CISO John O'Rourke explores how mature security programs can act as business enablers by reducing friction in sales and M&A processes while building long-term trust. It emphasizes the shift in buyer expectations and the competitive advantages gained through foundational security investments.

articles.title: Tracking drones with the 5G tower down the street
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a cost-effective method to detect drones in urban environments by repurposing 5G-Advanced base stations instead of deploying expensive radar or LiDAR hardware. This technique leverages existing telecommunications infrastructure to monitor aerial activity in real-time.

articles.title: Microsoft adds high-volume email sending to Exchange Online
articles.summary: Microsoft has introduced High Volume Email (HVE) in Exchange Online to allow organizations to send large quantities of automated, internal notifications without hitting standard messaging limits. This tenant-native feature is designed specifically for application-to-person communication, such as security alerts and payroll updates.

articles.title: Your customer passed authentication. So why are they sending money to a scammer?
articles.summary: This article explains how traditional identity and access management (IAM) systems fail to prevent fraud by only verifying users at login, advocating for continuous, behavioral-based authentication to detect threats throughout a session.

articles.title: AI recruiting biz Mercor says it was 'one of thousands' hit in LiteLLM supply-chain attack
articles.summary: AI recruiting platform Mercor confirmed it was among thousands of organizations impacted by a supply-chain attack targeting the LiteLLM library. The incident highlights the widespread risks associated with vulnerabilities in commonly used AI-integrated software dependencies.

articles.title: WhatsApp warns users of fake app used to distribute spyware
articles.summary: WhatsApp has identified a malicious iOS application developed by the Italian firm SIO, which is being used to distribute spyware targeting users primarily located in Italy.

articles.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in Google Dawn to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
articles.summary: CISA has added the use-after-free vulnerability CVE-2026-5281 in Google Dawn, which carries a CVSS score of 8.8, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog.

articles.title: New CrystalRAT malware adds RAT, stealer and prankware features
articles.summary: A new malware-as-a-service called CrystalRAT is being promoted on Telegram, featuring remote access, data theft, keylogging, clipboard hijacking, and prankware functionalities.

articles.title: Apple expands iOS 18 updates to more iPhones to block DarkSword attacks
articles.summary: No summary available.

articles.title: Hackers exploit TrueConf zero-day to push malicious software updates
articles.summary: Hackers are exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in TrueConf conference servers to distribute malicious software updates to connected endpoints. This flaw grants attackers the ability to execute arbitrary code across compromised systems.

articles.title: Apple Pushes Rare iOS 18 Patch for Devices at Risk from DarkSword Exploit
articles.summary: Apple has released an urgent iOS 18 security patch to mitigate risks associated with the DarkSword exploit, advising users to update their devices immediately. The recommendation also suggests transitioning to newer software versions for enhanced long-term security.

articles.title: Crypto platform Drift suspends services after millions stolen in security incident
articles.summary: The decentralized finance platform Drift has suspended operations following a massive cyberattack resulting in the theft of hundreds of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency. The platform confirmed the breach on Wednesday, prompting an immediate halt to its services.

articles.title: Google fixes fourth actively exploited Chrome zero-day of 2026
articles.summary: Google has patched a actively exploited zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-5281) in Chrome's WebGPU Dawn component, marking the fourth such incident in 2026. The flaw is a use-after-free bug that was addressed as part of a larger security update involving 21 vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Amazon security boss: AI makes pentesting 40% more efficient
articles.summary: Amazon's security leadership reports that integrating artificial intelligence into penetration testing workflows has improved efficiency by 40%. This highlights the growing role of automation in streamlining vulnerability assessment and security testing processes.

articles.title: Nissan says stolen data came from third-party vendor after hacking group claims breach
articles.summary: Nissan confirmed a third-party vendor’s file-transfer system was breached by a hacking group, though the company stated no customer data was compromised. The incident highlights the risks associated with third-party supply chain vulnerabilities.

articles.title: New EvilTokens service fuels Microsoft device code phishing attacks
articles.summary: The emergence of the EvilTokens toolkit facilitates device code phishing attacks, enabling threat actors to hijack Microsoft accounts and execute sophisticated business email compromise campaigns.

articles.title: Mercor confirms security incident tied to LiteLLM supply chain attack
articles.summary: Mercor confirmed a data breach resulting from a supply chain attack on the LiteLLM library, with the threat actor Lapsus$ claiming to have stolen hundreds of gigabytes of company data.

articles.title: Threat Brief: Widespread Impact of the Axios Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Unit 42 provides a detailed analysis of a significant supply chain attack against Axios, covering the complete execution lifecycle from initial dropper to post-incident cleanup.

articles.title: 'NoVoice' Android malware on Google Play infected 2.3 million devices
articles.summary: A malicious Android campaign dubbed 'NoVoice' infiltrated the Google Play Store, infecting over 50 apps that were collectively downloaded 2.3 million times. The malware highlights ongoing risks associated with malicious software bypassing official mobile app store vetting processes.

articles.title: North Dakota water treatment plant reports March ransomware attack
articles.summary: The city of Minot, North Dakota, reported a ransomware attack on its water treatment plant in March. Despite the incident, municipal officials confirmed that the facility's operations remain unaffected.

articles.title: LinkedIn Phishing Scam Uses Fake Notifications to Hijack Accounts
articles.summary: Threat actors are executing a phishing campaign that leverages fraudulent LinkedIn notifications and lookalike domains to compromise user credentials and gain unauthorized access to professional accounts. The attack aims to hijack profiles to harvest sensitive data from users.

articles.title: Cybercriminals take aim at Hasbro, weeks of recovery ahead
articles.summary: Hasbro has confirmed a cyberattack that forced the company to proactively take systems offline and initiate incident response protocols. The organization is currently working with third-party experts to assess the impact, with recovery efforts expected to span several weeks.

articles.title: CERT-UA Impersonation Campaign Spread AGEWHEEZE Malware to 1 Million Emails
articles.summary: Threat actor group UAC-0255 launched a massive phishing campaign impersonating CERT-UA to distribute the AGEWHEEZE remote administration tool via malicious ZIP archives. The campaign targeted approximately one million email accounts.

articles.title: Anthropic Leaks 512,000 Lines of Claude AI Code in Major Blunder
articles.summary: Anthropic inadvertently exposed over 512,000 lines of proprietary Claude AI code, including sensitive internal project data, due to human error. The incident has prompted the company to urge users to transition to a new native installer for security.

articles.title: European-Chinese geopolitical issues drive renewed cyberespionage campaign
articles.summary: Proofpoint researchers have identified a resurgence in cyberespionage activity by the threat group TA416, which is targeting European entities due to evolving geopolitical tensions with China. The group had previously shifted its focus away from the region before restarting these campaigns.

articles.title: North Korean hackers linked to Axios npm supply chain compromise
articles.summary: North Korean hackers compromised the popular Axios npm library by hijacking a maintainer's account to inject malicious post-install scripts. This supply chain attack allowed the attackers to deploy backdoored packages to unsuspecting developers.

articles.title: 'People's Panel' to check if UK wants controversial Digital ID will cost £630K
articles.summary: The UK government is allocating £630,000 for a public consultation panel to determine citizens' support for a controversial national digital identification system. This initiative highlights the ongoing policy debate surrounding digital identity implementation and privacy concerns.

articles.title: Microsoft Warns of WhatsApp-Delivered VBS Malware Hijacking Windows via UAC Bypass
articles.summary: Microsoft has identified a malicious campaign distributing VBS files via WhatsApp to establish persistence and remote access on Windows systems by bypassing User Account Control. The multi-stage infection chain targets Windows environments through deceptive social engineering lures.

articles.title: Routine Access Is Powering Modern Intrusions, a New Threat Report Finds
articles.summary: AI Summary Unavailable due to Gemini Rate Limit Hit - this will update upon rate reset

articles.title: Google links Axios npm supply chain attack to North Korea-linked APT UNC1069
articles.summary: Google has attributed a supply chain attack involving the Axios npm package to the North Korean-linked threat actor UNC1069, which targeted developers for financial gain.

articles.title: Exabeam expands ABA to detect AI agent threats across ChatGPT, Copilot, and Gemini
articles.summary: AI Summary Unavailable due to Gemini Rate Limit Hit - this will update upon rate reset

articles.title: Block the Prompt, Not the Work: The End of "Doctor No"
articles.summary: The article critiques the restrictive 'Doctor No' culture in enterprise security, arguing that CISOs must shift from blanket prohibitions of generative AI and collaboration tools toward enabling secure productivity in 2026.

articles.title: Ransomware Groups Exploit Legit IT Tools to Bypass Antivirus
articles.summary: Ransomware operators are increasingly utilizing legitimate administrative and IT utility software to evade antivirus detection and conduct malicious activities. This technique, known as the 'dual-use dilemma,' complicates defensive efforts by blending malicious actions with standard system management tools.

articles.title: Casbaneiro Phishing Targets Latin America and Europe Using Dynamic PDF Lures
articles.summary: The Brazilian threat actor group known as Augmented Marauder is distributing the Casbaneiro banking trojan across Latin America and Europe using sophisticated phishing campaigns and dynamic PDF lures. The attack chain utilizes the Horabot malware as a delivery mechanism to target Spanish-speaking corporate users.

articles.title: New Chrome Zero-Day CVE-2026-5281 Under Active Exploitation — Patch Released
articles.summary: Google has released a security update for Chrome to patch 21 vulnerabilities, including a high-severity, actively exploited use-after-free zero-day in the Dawn WebGPU component identified as CVE-2026-5281.

articles.title: FBI warns against using Chinese mobile apps due to privacy risks

articles.title: 3 Reasons Attackers Are Using Your Trusted Tools Against You (And Why You Don’t See It Coming)

articles.title: ImageMagick Zero-Day Enables RCE on Linux and WordPress Servers
articles.summary: A critical ImageMagick zero-day vulnerability has been discovered that allows for remote code execution on Linux and WordPress servers through malicious image uploads. The flaw successfully bypasses existing security policies, posing a significant risk to affected environments.

articles.title: Cybersecurity Firm TAC Security Hits 10,000 Clients, Enters Top 5 in Global VM & AppSec
articles.summary: TAC Security has achieved a significant milestone by reaching 10,000 clients and securing a position among the top five global providers for Vulnerability Management and Application Security.

articles.title: Defending Encryption in the Post Quantum Era

articles.title: SentinelOne autonomous detection blocks trojaned LiteLLM triggered by Claude Code
articles.summary: SentinelOne's AI security agent automatically blocked a supply chain attack involving a compromised LiteLLM package that was triggered via Claude Code. The incident demonstrates the effectiveness of autonomous detection in preventing malicious process execution on macOS systems.

articles.title: UK manufacturers under cyber fire with 80% reporting attacks
articles.summary: A significant majority of UK manufacturers, reaching 80%, have reported being the targets of cyberattacks, highlighting a widespread security crisis in the industrial sector.

articles.title: Free VPNs leak your data while claiming privacy
articles.summary: Many free Android VPN applications deceptively market privacy while harvesting user data and utilizing insecure infrastructure. These apps pose a significant security risk by tracking activity and requesting excessive permissions.

articles.title: Google Drive ransomware detection now on by default for paying users
articles.summary: Google has made its AI-powered Drive ransomware detection feature generally available and enabled by default for all paying users, enhancing cloud security.

articles.title: Claude Code Source Leaked via npm Packaging Error, Anthropic Confirms
articles.summary: Anthropic confirmed that the internal code for its AI coding assistant, Claude Code, was accidentally released due to a human error during npm packaging. The company assured that no sensitive customer data or credentials were involved or exposed in the incident.

articles.title: New Windows 11 emergency update fixes preview update install issues
articles.summary: Microsoft released an emergency update for Windows 11 to fix critical installation issues with the previously pulled March 2026 KB5079391 non-security preview update.

articles.title: Mimecast makes enterprise email security deployable in minutes
articles.summary: No summary available.

articles.title: Financial groups lay out a plan to fight AI identity attacks
articles.summary: No summary available.

articles.summary: No summary available.

articles.title: Claude Code source code accidentally leaked in NPM package
articles.summary: Anthropic accidentally leaked the source code for its closed-source Claude Code product via an NPM package, though the company stated no customer data or credentials were exposed in the incident.

articles.title: White House executive order purports to limit mail-in voting, mandate federal voter lists
articles.summary: No summary available.

articles.title: Google now allows you to change your @gmail.com address
articles.summary: Google is introducing a new feature in the U.S. that enables users to change their @gmail.com address or create an alias for their existing account.

articles.title: Proton launches new "Meet" privacy-focused conferencing platform
articles.summary: Proton has launched "Meet," a new privacy-focused video conferencing service positioned as an alternative to mainstream platforms like Google Meet, Zoom, and Microsoft Teams.

articles.title: GIGABYTE Control Center vulnerable to arbitrary file write flaw
articles.summary: The GIGABYTE Control Center software is affected by an arbitrary file-write vulnerability that could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to access files on vulnerable hosts.

articles.title: Claude AI finds Vim, Emacs RCE bugs that trigger on file open
articles.summary: Claude AI has identified remote code execution (RCE) vulnerabilities in Vim and GNU Emacs text editors. These bugs enable attackers to execute arbitrary code simply by tricking a user into opening a specially crafted file.

articles.title: Don't open that WhatsApp message, Microsoft warns
articles.summary: Microsoft has issued a warning advising users to exercise caution and avoid opening suspicious WhatsApp messages, indicating a potential threat or vulnerability associated with the platform.

articles.summary: Google is implementing mandatory Android developer verification for all developers to combat bad actors distributing harmful apps, with enforcement starting in select countries this September and expanding globally next year.

articles.summary: Cisco experienced a cyberattack where threat actors exploited credentials from a recent Trivy supply chain attack to breach its internal development environment and steal source code belonging to the company and its customers.

articles.summary: A zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-3502) in TrueConf video conferencing software is being actively exploited in attacks, dubbed "TrueChaos," targeting government entities in Southeast Asia.

articles.summary: Hackers compromised the npm account of the popular Axios JavaScript package, which has over 100M weekly downloads, to distribute cross-platform remote access trojans targeting Linux, Windows, and macOS systems.

articles.summary: The article discusses the accelerating cybersecurity landscape, driven by the weaponization of Artificial Intelligence by threat actors, which increases the speed of attacks and exploitation, making unified exposure management a critical boardroom priority.

articles.title: Silver Fox Expands Asia Cyber Campaign with AtlasCross RAT and Fake Domains
articles.summary: The 'Silver Fox' campaign is actively targeting Chinese-speaking users with typosquatted domains impersonating trusted software to deliver a new, previously undocumented remote access trojan called AtlasCross RAT.

articles.summary: The popular Axios npm package has suffered a supply chain attack, with malicious versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 introducing a fake dependency, "plain-crypto-js," that delivers a cross-platform Remote Access Trojan (RAT) targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux systems.

articles.summary: A credential-stealing campaign named DeepLoad is employing AI for sophisticated evasion techniques, including generating extensive code, logging keystrokes, and persistently re-infecting systems after being blocked.

articles.summary: OpenAI has patched a critical vulnerability in ChatGPT, discovered by Check Point, that allowed sensitive conversation data to be exfiltrated using malicious prompts, along with a separate Codex GitHub token vulnerability.

articles.summary: A new malware loader called DeepLoad is using the ClickFix social engineering tactic and WMI persistence to distribute itself. It employs AI-assisted obfuscation and process injection to evade detection, immediately stealing browser credentials and sessions.
